stem: Triangles PQR and LMN are graphed in the xy-plane. Triangle PQR has vertices P, Q, and R at (4, 5), (4, 7), and (6, 5), respectively. Triangle LMN has vertices L, M, and N at (4, 5), (4, 7 + k), and (6 + k, 5), respectively, where k is a positive constant. If the measure of ∠Q is t°, what is the measure of ∠N ?
explanation: Triangle PQR is a right isosceles triangle with the right angle at P, so ∠Q = ∠R = 45°, meaning t = 45. Triangle LMN is also a right isosceles triangle with the right angle at L, so ∠N = 45° as well; only (90 − t)° equals 45°.
